api/spec/packages/aip/src/currencies.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the v3 billing currencies API: fiat and custom currency models plus list/create operations, with a cost-bases/ child for per-currency rate records. All output compiles to api/v3/openapi.yaml and the Go/JS/Python SDKs via make gen-api; no hand-written Go or OpenAPI lives here.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none
patterns.description: Currency is @discriminated with envelope 'none' and discriminatorPropertyName 'type'; new variants must extend the union, never add a flat polymorphic field.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Currency { fiat: CurrencyFiat, custom: CurrencyCustom }

patterns.name: Generic base model CurrencyBase<T>
patterns.description: Type-specific models spread CurrencyBase<CurrencyType.X> and add only their own fields; common fields (id, type, name, description, symbol) live only on CurrencyBase.

patterns.name: @visibility on every field
patterns.description: All fields carry explicit @visibility(Lifecycle.*); read-only system fields use Lifecycle.Read only, user-settable use Lifecycle.Create and/or Read.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every exported type
patterns.description: Every model, enum, union, and scalar has @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") to stabilize generated SDK type names and prevent collisions.

patterns.name: Stability @extension decorators on every operation
patterns.description: Every operation carries @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true) and @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true); omitting either breaks API maturity tracking.

patterns.name: PagePaginationQuery + deepObject filter
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery and accept filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCurrenciesParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in currency.tsp, operations in operations.tsp
patterns.description: currency.tsp defines models with no @typespec/http import; operations.tsp imports HTTP and declares the interfaces; index.tsp orders all imports including cost-bases/.

key_file_guides.role: CurrencyType enum, Currency discriminated union, CurrencyBase<T> generic, CurrencyFiat, CurrencyCustom, CurrencyCodeCustom scalar. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding @get/@post or importing @typespec/http breaks model/operation separation; missing @friendlyName destabilizes SDK names; missing @visibility defaults to all lifecycle phases.

key_file_guides.role: CurrenciesOperations (list) and CurrenciesCustomOperations (create) with HTTP + stability decorators and ListCurrenciesParamsFilter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting @extension stability decorators breaks maturity tracking; using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery causes contract drift.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point importing currency.tsp, operations.tsp, and cost-bases/operations.tsp in order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files in this folder must be imported here or they are silently excluded from compilation.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml instead of regenerating via make gen-api
Declaring fields without @visibility — they default to all lifecycle phases and leak write-only/system fields into create payloads
Using a @friendlyName that duplicates an existing model name — causes SDK type collisions
Importing @typespec/http in currency.tsp — HTTP decorators belong only in operations.tsp

decisions.decision: Currency is a discriminated union (envelope:none) rather than a flat polymorphic model
decisions.rationale: Enables SDK discriminator deserialization without a wrapper envelope field, matching the fiat/custom split at the type level.

decisions.decision: Models and operations split across currency.tsp and operations.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps model definitions free of HTTP concerns so they can be imported without pulling in HTTP decorator dependencies.
The currencies API is internal/unstable; the decorators gate exposure in public OpenAPI outputs and SDK generation.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new custom currency variant with a create operation

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers.purpose: Root TypeSpec folder for the v3 customer API: core customer CRUD, billing overlay, Stripe session operations, and the credits/ and charges/ sub-domains. Coordinates sub-domain imports via index.tsp and is the entry point for the Customers namespace; compiles to api/v3/openapi.yaml and SDKs via make gen-api.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for mutable entities
patterns.description: Core CRUD entities (Customer) spread ...Shared.Resource to inherit id, name, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at, metadata.

patterns.name: @visibility lists every applicable lifecycle
patterns.description: Each field explicitly lists Create/Read/Update where applicable; omitting Update for a mutable field excludes it from PUT/upsert payloads.

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on every exported type
patterns.description: All exported models, enums, unions, and scalars carry a Billing-prefixed @friendlyName to stabilize SDK names.

patterns.name: Shared generic request/response wrappers
patterns.description: Operations use Shared.CreateRequest<T>, UpsertRequest<T>, CreateResponse<T>, GetResponse<T>, UpsertResponse<T> rather than bare model types.

patterns.name: deepObject filter + PagePaginationQuery for lists
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery and accept filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCustomersParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in domain .tsp, HTTP in operations.tsp
patterns.description: customer.tsp and billing.tsp define models only; operations.tsp imports @typespec/http and declares all interfaces. Stripe sub-operations use @route sub-paths.

patterns.name: Sub-domains as child folders imported via index.tsp
patterns.description: credits/ and charges/ each have their own index.tsp imported in the parent index.tsp; new sub-domains follow the same pattern.

key_file_guides.role: Customer, Address, CustomerUsageAttribution, CustomerReference, CustomerKeyReference, UsageAttributionSubjectKey. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing @visibility defaults to all lifecycles; not spreading Shared.Resource on new CRUD entities breaks consistency; no HTTP decorators here.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerBillingData referencing Billing.BillingProfileReference and Apps.AppCustomerData — bridges customer, billing, and apps namespaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep billing concerns here, not in customer.tsp; it imports apps and billing index.tsp by design.

key_file_guides.role: CustomersOperations (CRUD + list), CustomerBillingOperations (get/upsert billing, app-data, Stripe checkout/portal sessions), and related request models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each operation needs @operationId and @summary; Stripe sub-ops use @route; responses must include Common.ErrorResponses.

key_file_guides.role: Root import file pulling in customer.tsp, operations.tsp, credits/index.tsp, charges/index.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New sub-domain folders must be imported here or they are silently excluded from compilation.

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) in customer.tsp or billing.tsp — they belong only in operations.tsp
Omitting @visibility on fields — they default to all lifecycle phases and leak write-only/system fields into payloads
Omitting @friendlyName on new models or enums — the generated SDK name becomes unstable or collides
Using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml instead of running make gen-api

decisions.decision: Customer billing and app data live in billing.tsp, not customer.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps core customer identity free of billing/app coupling; billing.tsp bridges namespaces explicitly so the dependency is traceable.

decisions.decision: credits/ and charges/ are child sub-folders imported via index.tsp
decisions.rationale: Maintains logical separation of sub-domains while keeping the compilation path unified through the parent index.

decisions.decision: Stripe operations use @route sub-paths within CustomerBillingOperations
decisions.rationale: Co-locates Stripe session operations with the billing interface they belong to, avoiding a proliferation of top-level interfaces.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new customer sub-resource with list and get operations
